questions.question_text_full:
Select the most appropriate ANTONYM of the given word.
Thwart
questions.notes: No answer marked on the page.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.
Smriti greeted me and asked me where I was working then.

options.text: Smriti said to me, “Hello! Where were you working then?”
Smriti said to me, “Hello! Where I was working then?”
Smriti said to me, “Hello! Where are you working now?”
Smriti said to me, “Hello! Where you are working now?”

We yet / have time / to catch / the bus.

questions.question_text_full:
Select the option that expresses the given sentence in direct speech.
Raza requested his parents to forgive him that time and promised never to play truant again.

options.text: Raza said to his parents, “Please forgive me this time. I promise never to play truant again.”
Raza said to his parents, “Forgive me that time. I promise I would never play truant again.”
Raza said to his parents, “Please forgive me that time. I promise to never play truant again.”
Raza said to his parents, “Forgive me this time. I promised never to play truant again.”

questions.question_text_full:
Sentences of a paragraph are given below in jumbled order. Arrange the sentences in the correct order to form a meaningful and coherent paragraph.
A. There are several factors that contribute to wisdom.
B. Doing this has become more difficult than before owing to the complexity of the specialised knowledge required.
C. This is the capacity to take account of all important factors in a problem and to attach to each its due weight.
D. Of these, I should put first a sense of proportion.
questions.notes: No answer marked on the page.

In case no improvement is needed, select ‘No improvement required’.
Megha’s habit of procrastination puts her colleagues on lot of trouble.
